channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: DOJ releases Special Counsel Jack Smith's report on investigation into Trump election interference case
item.description: The first part of Special Counsel Jack Smith’s report on his now-closed investigations into President-elect Donald Trump was released Tuesday, days before he will be sworn into office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Trump's nominee for defense secretary, faces crucial test at confirmation hearing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s choice for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, is heading into a potentially explosive confirmation hearing Tuesday as senators question whether the former combat veteran and TV news show host is fit to lead the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An American tradition: Defeated candidates attending the president-elect's inauguration
item.description: In January 1981, Jimmy Carter nodded politely toward Ronald Reagan as the new Republican president thanked the Democrat for his administration's help after Reagan resoundingly defeated Carter the previous November.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley bill past second hurdle
item.description: The Laken Riley Act on Monday cleared a second procedural hurdle in the Senate as talk turns to an amendment push by Democrats in order to potentially make alterations to the bill. Senators voted 82-10 on the motion to proceed to the legislation, allowing the chamber to begin debate.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutor who investigated Hunter Biden defends probes, denounces president's remarks in new report
item.description: The special counsel who brought criminal charges against Hunter Biden says the probes were “the culmination of thorough, impartial investigations, not partisan politics.”.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis calls special session to implement Trump's immigration policies
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is calling state lawmakers into a special session to help carry out President-elect Donald Trump's promises for a swift crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First hearing in 'Trump-proof' California special session canceled as chairman's district hit by wildfires
item.description: A California Assembly committee chairman canceled the planned first hearing in a special "Trump-proof" session due to wildfires ravaging his legislative district.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A tough-on-crime approach is back in US state capitols
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Within minutes of his inauguration Monday, new Missouri Gov. Mike Kehoe unleashed a volley of orders targeting crime. The tone-setting move reflects a national trend. After a period of relaxed sentencing laws, a tough-on-crime approach is back in political favor in the U.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden: Govt. to give $770 to each resident affected by LA wildfires
item.description: President Joe Biden convened senior officials to discuss the federal response to the LA County fires.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Cannon allows for release of Smith report on Trump election interference
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon on Monday allowed the release of the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s efforts to block the peaceful transfer of power after the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carrie Underwood, Village People to perform at Trump's inauguration events
item.description: Country music star Carrie Underwood will perform “America the Beautiful” at Donald Trump’s inauguration next week and the 1970s hitmakers Village People will perform at two inaugural events.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge clears way for release of special counsel report on Trump election case
item.description: Special Counsel Jack Smith's report on his investigation into President-elect Trump for election interference is cleared to be released to the public.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to watch Trump's inauguration on NewsNation
item.description: Donald Trump will be inaugurated as the 47th U.S. president on Jan. 20 for his second nonconsecutive term.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Melania Trump says she's packed and ready for the move back into the White House
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Incoming first lady Melania Trump says she's packed and ready to move back into the White House, where son Barron will have a bedroom, and she plans to revive her Be Best children's initiative.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth's views on women in combat, infidelity and more
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth will try to convince members of the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday that he should be the next secretary of defense, following weeks of meetings with lawmakers who peppered him with questions about his character and views on a range of military issues.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CA firefighters face impersonators, staffing shortages, low pay
item.description: LA County Sheriff Robert Luna said he encountered a man dressed as a firefighter burglarizing a home.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Steve Bannon says he will ‘take down’ the ‘truly evil’ Elon Musk
item.description: “I will have Elon Musk run out of here by Inauguration Day,” Bannon said, adding “He is a truly evil guy, a very bad guy. I made it my personal thing to take this guy down.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans face massive debt problem
item.description: House Republicans have a problem. They want to pass a massive agenda for President-elect Trump, preferably in his first 100 days of office. And they don’t want to add to the federal deficit. That looks impossible.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: LA fire chief says city failed department amid ongoing wildfires
item.description: Crowley said that when firefighters operate in the field, they expect to have water, but if there isn’t any available, “they’re going to go find water.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: US streamlines deportation flights to China
item.description: The Biden administration repatriated more than 500 Chinese nationals in the last year, laying the groundwork for systematic deportations across the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Congress braces for California wildfire fallout
item.description: House lawmakers are bracing for what could be a contentious battle over emergency spending in the wake of the wildfires that are tearing through Southern California — an unprecedented disaster that’s already estimated to have caused more than $50 billion in damage in and around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: LA Times owner: It's 'crazy' we don't elect people with competence
item.description: Los Angeles Times owner Dr. Pat Soon-Shiong said it's "crazy" his state "doesn't elect leaders with competence.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Trump vows to shed light on mystery drones when president
item.description: Trump, who will be inaugurated as the 47th president on Jan. 20, criticized the Biden administration's handling of the mystery drones.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: President-elect Trump sentenced to unconditional discharge in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pinellas residents struggle with permit delays, seek accountability
item.description: Many Pinellas County residents have been displaced from their homes since Hurricane Helene hit more than 100 days ago. Some have nowhere else to go, forcing them to stay in damaged and unfinished homes until the county gives them the permits to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
